note: [PROPOSAL ruling, KTTL Chunk 2 reconciliation]: package_type/required/optional are derived from the real config.KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_TEMPLATES['Dashboard'] profile as it now reads -- required=[Process, Task, System, Dependency, Control, Escalation] (6 at Critical weight 3 = 18), optional=[Known Issue] (1 at Supporting weight 1) = 19 total denominator points. The prior profile (Process, Task, System, Business Rule required; Risk optional) is stale; Business Rule and Risk are no longer part of the Dashboard profile at all. The extraction_mock was redesigned so the graph scores cleanly as pure Dashboard (no blending with Operations, which now shares 5 of 6 required types with Dashboard) -- see services/kttl.py BLEND_SCORE_GAP/BLEND_MIN_SCORE: omitting System entirely (as the old fixture did) made Dashboard's required_recall drop enough to fall inside the blend gap against Operations, so System is now present-but-Partial (empty description) instead of Missing. Escalation is intentionally left Partial and Known Issue intentionally left Missing in the final state (no gap-answer step closes either) so the dataset's final coverage lands below 100%.
